CSE5313CSE5313 Coding and information theory for data science (Lecture 24)

CSE5313 Coding and information theory for data science (Lecture 24)

Continue on coded computing

Matrix-vector multiplication: y=Axy=Axy=Ax, where A∈FM×N,x∈FNA\in \mathbb{F}^{M\times N},x\in \mathbb{F}^NA∈FM×N,x∈FN

  • MDS codes.
    • Recover threshold K=MK=MK=M.
  • Short-dot codes.
    • Recover threshold K≥MK\geq MK≥M.
    • Every node receives at most s=P−K+MPs=\frac{P-K+M}{P}s=PP−K+M​. NNN elements of xxx.

Matrix-matrix multiplication

Problem Formulation:

  • A=[A0A1…AM−1]∈FL×LA=[A_0 A_1\ldots A_{M-1}]\in \mathbb{F}^{L\times L}A=[A0​A1​…AM−1​]∈FL×L, B=[B0,B1,…,BM−1]∈FL×LB=[B_0,B_1,\ldots,B_{M-1}]\in \mathbb{F}^{L\times L}B=[B0​,B1​,…,BM−1​]∈FL×L
  • Am,BmA_m,B_mAm​,Bm​ are submatrices of A,BA,BA,B.
  • We want to compute C=A⊤BC=A^\top BC=A⊤B.

Trivial solution:

  • Index each worker node by m,n∈[0,M−1]m,n\in [0,M-1]m,n∈[0,M−1].
  • Worker node (m,n)(m,n)(m,n) performs matrix multiplication Am⊤⋅BnA_m^\top\cdot B_nAm⊤​⋅Bn​.
  • Need P=M2P=M^2P=M2 nodes.
  • No erasure tolerance.

Can we do better?

1-D MDS Method

Create [A~0,A~1,…,A~S−1][\tilde{A}_0,\tilde{A}_1,\ldots,\tilde{A}_{S-1}][A~0​,A~1​,…,A~S−1​] by encoding [A0,A1,…,AM−1][A_0,A_1,\ldots,A_{M-1}][A0​,A1​,…,AM−1​]. with some (S,M)(S,M)(S,M) MDS code.

Need P=SMP=SMP=SM worker nodes, and index each one by s∈[0,S−1],n∈[0,M−1]s\in [0,S-1], n\in [0,M-1]s∈[0,S−1],n∈[0,M−1].

Worker node (s,n)(s,n)(s,n) performs matrix multiplication A~s⊤⋅Bn\tilde{A}_s^\top\cdot B_nA~s⊤​⋅Bn​.

[A0⊤A1⊤A0⊤+A1⊤][B0B1]\begin{bmatrix} A_0^\top\\ A_1^\top\\ A_0^\top+A_1^\top \end{bmatrix} \begin{bmatrix} B_0 & B_1 \end{bmatrix}​A0⊤​A1⊤​A0⊤​+A1⊤​​​[B0​​B1​​]

Need S−MS-MS−M responses from each column.

The recovery threshold K=P−S+MK=P-S+MK=P−S+M nodes.

This is trivially parity check code with 1 recovery threshold.

2-D MDS Method

Encode [A0,A1,…,AM−1][A_0,A_1,\ldots,A_{M-1}][A0​,A1​,…,AM−1​] with some (S,M)(S,M)(S,M) MDS code.

Encode [B0,B1,…,BM−1][B_0,B_1,\ldots,B_{M-1}][B0​,B1​,…,BM−1​] with some (S,M)(S,M)(S,M) MDS code.

Need P=S2P=S^2P=S2 nodes.

[A0⊤A1⊤A0⊤+A1⊤][B0B1B0+B1]\begin{bmatrix} A_0^\top\\ A_1^\top\\ A_0^\top+A_1^\top \end{bmatrix} \begin{bmatrix} B_0 & B_1 & B_0+B_1 \end{bmatrix}​A0⊤​A1⊤​A0⊤​+A1⊤​​​[B0​​B1​​B0​+B1​​]

Decodability depends on the pattern.

  • Consider an S×SS\times SS×S bipartite graph (rows on left, columns on right).
  • Draw an (i,j)(i,j)(i,j) edge if A~i⊤⋅B~j\tilde{A}_i^\top\cdot \tilde{B}_jA~i⊤​⋅B~j​ is missing
  • Row iii is decodable if and only if the degree of iii‘th left node ≤S−M\leq S-M≤S−M.
  • Column jjj is decodable if and only if the degree of jjj‘th right node ≤S−M\leq S-M≤S−M.

Peeling algorithm:

  • Traverse the graph.
  • If ∃v\exists v∃v,deg⁡v≤S−M\deg v\leq S-Mdegv≤S−M, remove edges.
  • Repeat.

Corollary:

  • A pattern is decodable if and only if the above graph does not contain a subgraph with all degree larger than S−MS-MS−M.
Note
  1. K1D−MDS=P−S+M=Θ(P)K_{1D-MDS}=P-S+M=\Theta(P)K1D−MDS​=P−S+M=Θ(P) (linearly)
  2. K2D−MDS=P−(S−M+1)2+1K_{2D-MDS}=P-(S-M+1)^2+1K2D−MDS​=P−(S−M+1)2+1.
    • Consider S×SS\times SS×S bipartite graph with (S−M+1)×(S−M+1)(S-M+1)\times (S-M+1)(S−M+1)×(S−M+1) complete subgraph.
    • There exists subgraph with all degrees larger than S−M  ⟹  S-M\impliesS−M⟹ not decodable.
    • On the other hand: Fewer than (S−M+1)2(S-M+1)^2(S−M+1)2 edges cannot form a subgraph with all degrees >S−M>S-M>S−M.
    • KKK scales sub-linearly with PPP.
  3. Kproduct<P−M2=S2−M2=Θ(P)K_{product}<P-M^2=S^2-M^2=\Theta(\sqrt{P})Kproduct​<P−M2=S2−M2=Θ(P​)

Our goal is to get rid of PPP.

Polynomial codes

Polynomial representation

Coefficient representation of a polynomial:

  • f(x)=fdxd+fd−1xd−1+⋯+f1x+f0f(x)=f_dx^d+f_{d-1}x^{d-1}+\cdots+f_1x+f_0f(x)=fd​xd+fd−1​xd−1+⋯+f1​x+f0​
  • Uniquely defined by coefficients [fd,fd−1,…,f0][f_d,f_{d-1},\ldots,f_0][fd​,fd−1​,…,f0​].

Value presentation of a polynomial:

  • Theorem: A polynomial of degree ddd is uniquely determined by d+1d+1d+1 points.
  • Proof Outline: First create a polynomial of degree ddd from the d+1d+1d+1 points using Lagrange interpolation, and show such polynomial is unique.
  • Uniquely defined by evaluations [(α1,f(α1)),…,(αd,f(αd))][(\alpha_1,f(\alpha_1)),\ldots,(\alpha_{d},f(\alpha_{d}))][(α1​,f(α1​)),…,(αd​,f(αd​))]

Why should we want value representation?

  • With coefficient representation, polynomial product takes O(d2)O(d^2)O(d2) multiplications.
  • With value representation, polynomial product takes 2d+12d+12d+1 multiplications.

Definition of a polynomial code

link to paper 

Problem formulation:

A=[A0,A1,…,AM−1]∈FL×L,B=[B0,B1,…,BM−1]∈FL×LA=[A_0,A_1,\ldots,A_{M-1}]\in \mathbb{F}^{L\times L}, B=[B_0,B_1,\ldots,B_{M-1}]\in \mathbb{F}^{L\times L}A=[A0​,A1​,…,AM−1​]∈FL×L,B=[B0​,B1​,…,BM−1​]∈FL×L

We want to compute C=A⊤BC=A^\top BC=A⊤B.

Define matrix polynomials:

pA(x)=∑i=0M−1Aixip_A(x)=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1} A_i x^ipA​(x)=∑i=0M−1​Ai​xi, degree M−1M-1M−1

pB(x)=∑i=0M−1BixiMp_B(x)=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1} B_i x^{iM}pB​(x)=∑i=0M−1​Bi​xiM, degree M(M−1)M(M-1)M(M−1)

where each Ai,BiA_i,B_iAi​,Bi​ are matrices

We have

h(x)=pA(x)pB(x)=∑i=0M−1∑j=0M−1AiBjxi+jMh(x)=p_A(x)p_B(x)=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\sum_{j=0}^{M-1} A_i B_j x^{i+jM}h(x)=pA​(x)pB​(x)=i=0∑M−1​j=0∑M−1​Ai​Bj​xi+jM

deg⁡h(x)≤M(M−1)+M−1=M2−1\deg h(x)\leq M(M-1)+M-1=M^2-1degh(x)≤M(M−1)+M−1=M2−1

Observe that

xi1+j1M=xi2+j2Mx^{i_1+j_1M}=x^{i_2+j_2M}xi1​+j1​M=xi2​+j2​M

if and only if m1=n1m_1=n_1m1​=n1​ and m2=n2m_2=n_2m2​=n2​.

The coefficient of xi+jMx^{i+jM}xi+jM is Ai⊤BjA_i^\top B_jAi⊤​Bj​.

Computing C=A⊤BC=A^\top BC=A⊤B is equivalent to find the coefficient representation of h(x)h(x)h(x).

Encoding of polynomial codes

The master choose ω0,ω1,…,ωP−1∈F\omega_0,\omega_1,\ldots,\omega_{P-1}\in \mathbb{F}ω0​,ω1​,…,ωP−1​∈F.

  • Note that this requires ∣F∣≥P|\mathbb{F}|\geq P∣F∣≥P.

For every node i∈[0,P−1]i\in [0,P-1]i∈[0,P−1], the master computes A~i=pA(ωi)\tilde{A}_i=p_A(\omega_i)A~i​=pA​(ωi​)

  • Equivalent to multiplying [A0⊤,A1⊤,…,AM−1⊤][A_0^\top,A_1^\top,\ldots,A_{M-1}^\top][A0⊤​,A1⊤​,…,AM−1⊤​] by Vandermonde matrix over ω0,ω1,…,ωP−1\omega_0,\omega_1,\ldots,\omega_{P-1}ω0​,ω1​,…,ωP−1​.
  • Can be speed up using FFT.

Similarly, the master computes B~i=pB(ωi)\tilde{B}_i=p_B(\omega_i)B~i​=pB​(ωi​) for every node i∈[0,P−1]i\in [0,P-1]i∈[0,P−1].

Every node i∈[0,P−1]i\in [0,P-1]i∈[0,P−1] computes and returns ci=pA(ωi)pB(ωi)c_i=p_A(\omega_i)p_B(\omega_i)ci​=pA​(ωi​)pB​(ωi​) to the master.

cic_ici​ is the evaluation of polynomial h(x)=pA(x)pB(x)h(x)=p_A(x)p_B(x)h(x)=pA​(x)pB​(x) at ωi\omega_iωi​.

Recall that h(x)=∑i=0M−1∑j=0M−1Ai⊤Bjxi+jMh(x)=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\sum_{j=0}^{M-1} A_i^\top B_j x^{i+jM}h(x)=∑i=0M−1​∑j=0M−1​Ai⊤​Bj​xi+jM.

  • Computing C=A⊤BC=A^\top BC=A⊤B is equivalent to finding the coefficient representation of h(x)h(x)h(x).

Recall that a polynomial of degree ddd can be uniquely defined by d+1d+1d+1 points.

  • With MNMNMN evaluations of h(x)h(x)h(x), we can recover the coefficient representation for polynomial h(x)h(x)h(x).

The recovery threshold K=M2K=M^2K=M2, independent of PPP, the number of worker nodes.

Done.

MatDot Codes

link to paper 

Problem formulation:

  • We want to compute C=A⊤BC=A^\top BC=A⊤B.

  • Unlike polynomial codes, we let A=[A0A1⋮AM−1]A=\begin{bmatrix} A_0\\ A_1\\ \vdots\\ A_{M-1} \end{bmatrix}A=​A0​A1​⋮AM−1​​​ and B=[B0B1⋮BM−1]B=\begin{bmatrix} B_0\\ B_1\\ \vdots\\ B_{M-1} \end{bmatrix}B=​B0​B1​⋮BM−1​​​. And A,B∈FL×LA,B\in \mathbb{F}^{L\times L}A,B∈FL×L.

  • In polynomial codes, A=[A0A1…AM−1]A=\begin{bmatrix} A_0 A_1\ldots A_{M-1} \end{bmatrix}A=[A0​A1​…AM−1​​] and B=[B0B1…BM−1]B=\begin{bmatrix} B_0 B_1\ldots B_{M-1} \end{bmatrix}B=[B0​B1​…BM−1​​].

Key observation:

Am⊤A_m^\topAm⊤​ is an L×LML\times \frac{L}{M}L×ML​ matrix, and BmB_mBm​ is an LM×L\frac{L}{M}\times LML​×L matrix. Hence, Am⊤BmA_m^\top B_mAm⊤​Bm​ is an L×LL\times LL×L matrix.

Let C=A⊤B=∑m=0M−1Am⊤BmC=A^\top B=\sum_{m=0}^{M-1} A_m^\top B_mC=A⊤B=∑m=0M−1​Am⊤​Bm​.

Let pA(x)=∑m=0M−1Amxmp_A(x)=\sum_{m=0}^{M-1} A_m x^mpA​(x)=∑m=0M−1​Am​xm, degree M−1M-1M−1.

Let pB(x)=∑m=0M−1Bmxmp_B(x)=\sum_{m=0}^{M-1} B_m x^mpB​(x)=∑m=0M−1​Bm​xm, degree M−1M-1M−1.

Both have degree M−1M-1M−1.

And h(x)=pA(x)pB(x)h(x)=p_A(x)p_B(x)h(x)=pA​(x)pB​(x).

deg⁡h(x)≤M−1+M−1=2M−2\deg h(x)\leq M-1+M-1=2M-2degh(x)≤M−1+M−1=2M−2

Key observation:

  • The coefficient of the term xM−1x^{M-1}xM−1 in h(x)h(x)h(x) is ∑m=0M−1Am⊤Bm\sum_{m=0}^{M-1} A_m^\top B_m∑m=0M−1​Am⊤​Bm​.

Recall that C=A⊤B=∑m=0M−1Am⊤BmC=A^\top B=\sum_{m=0}^{M-1} A_m^\top B_mC=A⊤B=∑m=0M−1​Am⊤​Bm​.

Finding this coefficient is equivalent to finding the result of A⊤BA^\top BA⊤B.

Here we sacrifice the bandwidth of the network for the computational power.

General Scheme for MatDot Codes

The master choose ω0,ω1,…,ωP−1∈F\omega_0,\omega_1,\ldots,\omega_{P-1}\in \mathbb{F}ω0​,ω1​,…,ωP−1​∈F.

  • Note that this requires ∣F∣≥P|\mathbb{F}|\geq P∣F∣≥P.

For every node i∈[0,P−1]i\in [0,P-1]i∈[0,P−1], the master computes A~i=pA(ωi)\tilde{A}_i=p_A(\omega_i)A~i​=pA​(ωi​) and B~i=pB(ωi)\tilde{B}_i=p_B(\omega_i)B~i​=pB​(ωi​).

  • pA(x)=∑m=0M−1Amxmp_A(x)=\sum_{m=0}^{M-1} A_m x^mpA​(x)=∑m=0M−1​Am​xm, degree M−1M-1M−1.
  • pB(x)=∑m=0M−1Bmxmp_B(x)=\sum_{m=0}^{M-1} B_m x^mpB​(x)=∑m=0M−1​Bm​xm, degree M−1M-1M−1.

The master sends A~i,B~i\tilde{A}_i,\tilde{B}_iA~i​,B~i​ to node iii.

Every node i∈[0,P−1]i\in [0,P-1]i∈[0,P−1] computes and returns ci=pA(ωi)pB(ωi)c_i=p_A(\omega_i)p_B(\omega_i)ci​=pA​(ωi​)pB​(ωi​) to the master.

The master needs deg⁡h(x)+1=2M−1\deg h(x)+1=2M-1degh(x)+1=2M−1 evaluations to obtain h(x)h(x)h(x).

  • The recovery threshold is K=2M−1K=2M-1K=2M−1

Recap on Matrix-Matrix multiplication

A,B∈FL×LA,B\in \mathbb{F}^{L\times L}A,B∈FL×L, we want to compute C=A⊤BC=A^\top BC=A⊤B with PPP nodes.

Every node receives 1m\frac{1}{m}m1​ of AAA and 1m\frac{1}{m}m1​ of BBB.

CodeRecovery threshold KKK
1D-MDSΘ(P)\Theta(P)Θ(P)
2D-MDS≤Θ(P)\leq \Theta(\sqrt{P})≤Θ(P​)
Polynomial codesΘ(M2)\Theta(M^2)Θ(M2)
MatDot codesΘ(M)\Theta(M)Θ(M)

Polynomial Evaluation

Problem formulation:

  • We have KKK datasets X1,X2,…,XKX_1,X_2,\ldots,X_KX1​,X2​,…,XK​.
  • Want to compute some polynomial function fff of degree ddd on each dataset.
    • Want f(X1),f(X2),…,f(XK)f(X_1),f(X_2),\ldots,f(X_K)f(X1​),f(X2​),…,f(XK​).
  • Examples:
    • X1,X2,…,XKX_1,X_2,\ldots,X_KX1​,X2​,…,XK​ are points in FM×M\mathbb{F}^{M\times M}FM×M, and f(X)=X8+3X2+1f(X)=X^8+3X^2+1f(X)=X8+3X2+1.
    • Xk=(Xk(1),Xk(2))X_k=(X_k^{(1)},X_k^{(2)})Xk​=(Xk(1)​,Xk(2)​), both in FM×M\mathbb{F}^{M\times M}FM×M, and f(X)=Xk(1)Xk(2)f(X)=X_k^{(1)}X_k^{(2)}f(X)=Xk(1)​Xk(2)​.
    • Gradient computation.

PPP worker nodes:

  • Some are stragglers, i.e., not responsive.
  • Some are adversaries, i.e., return erroneous results.
  • Privacy: We do not want to expose datasets to worker nodes.

Replication code

Suppose P=(r+1)⋅KP=(r+1)\cdot KP=(r+1)⋅K.

  • Partition the PPP nodes to KKK groups of size r+1r+1r+1 each.
  • Node in group iii computes and returns f(Xi)f(X_i)f(Xi​) to the master.
  • Replication tolerates rrr stragglers, or ⌊r2⌋\lfloor \frac{r}{2} \rfloor⌊2r​⌋ adversaries.

Linear codes

Recall previous linear computations (matrix-vector):

  • [A~1,A~2,A~3]=[A1,A2,A1+A2][\tilde{A}_1,\tilde{A}_2,\tilde{A}_3]=[A_1,A_2,A_1+A_2][A~1​,A~2​,A~3​]=[A1​,A2​,A1​+A2​] is the corresponding codeword of [A1,A2][A_1,A_2][A1​,A2​].
  • Every worker node iii computes f(A~i)=A~ixf(\tilde{A}_i)=\tilde{A}_i xf(A~i​)=A~i​x.
  • [A~1x,A~2x,A~3x]=[A1x,A2x,A1x+A2x][\tilde{A}_1x, \tilde{A}_2x, \tilde{A}_3x]=[A_1x,A_2x,A_1x+A_2x][A~1​x,A~2​x,A~3​x]=[A1​x,A2​x,A1​x+A2​x] is the corresponding codeword of [A1x,A2x][A_1x,A_2x][A1​x,A2​x].
    • This enables to decode [A1x,A2x][A_1x,A_2x][A1​x,A2​x] from [A~1x,A~2x,A~3x][\tilde{A}_1x,\tilde{A}_2x,\tilde{A}_3 x][A~1​x,A~2​x,A~3​x].

However, fff is a polynomial of degree ddd, not a linear transformation unless d=1d=1d=1.

  • f(cX)≠cf(X)f(cX)\neq cf(X)f(cX)=cf(X), where ccc is a constant.
  • f(X1+X2)≠f(X1)+f(X2)f(X_1+X_2)\neq f(X_1)+f(X_2)f(X1​+X2​)=f(X1​)+f(X2​).
Caution

[f(X~1),f(X~2),…,f(X~K)][f(\tilde{X}_1),f(\tilde{X}_2),\ldots,f(\tilde{X}_K)][f(X~1​),f(X~2​),…,f(X~K​)] is not the codeword corresponding to [f(X1),f(X2),…,f(XK)][f(X_1),f(X_2),\ldots,f(X_K)][f(X1​),f(X2​),…,f(XK​)] in any linear code.

Our goal is to create an encoder/decode such that:

  • Linear encoding: is the codeword of [X1,X2,…,XK][X_1,X_2,\ldots,X_K][X1​,X2​,…,XK​] for some linear code.
    • i.e., [X~1,X~2,…,X~K]=[X1,X2,…,XK]G[\tilde{X}_1,\tilde{X}_2,\ldots,\tilde{X}_K]=[X_1,X_2,\ldots,X_K]G[X~1​,X~2​,…,X~K​]=[X1​,X2​,…,XK​]G for some generator matrix GGG.
    • Every X~i\tilde{X}_iX~i​ is some linear combination of X1,…,XKX_1,\ldots,X_KX1​,…,XK​.
  • The f(Xi)f(X_i)f(Xi​) are decodable from some subset of f(X~i)f(\tilde{X}_i)f(X~i​)‘s.
    • Some of coded results are missing, erroneous.
  • XiX_iXi​’s are kept private.

Lagrange Coded Computing

Let ℓ(z)\ell(z)ℓ(z) be a polynomial whose evaluations at ω1,…,ωK\omega_1,\ldots,\omega_{K}ω1​,…,ωK​ are X1,…,XKX_1,\ldots,X_KX1​,…,XK​.

  • That is, ℓ(ωi)=Xi\ell(\omega_i)=X_iℓ(ωi​)=Xi​ for every ωi∈F,i∈[K]\omega_i\in \mathbb{F}, i\in [K]ωi​∈F,i∈[K].

Some example constructions:

Given X1,…,XKX_1,\ldots,X_KX1​,…,XK​ with corresponding ω1,…,ωK\omega_1,\ldots,\omega_Kω1​,…,ωK​

  • ℓ(z)=∑i=1KXiLi(z)\ell(z)=\sum_{i=1}^K X_iL_i(z)ℓ(z)=∑i=1K​Xi​Li​(z), where Li(z)=∏j∈[K],j≠iz−ωjωi−ωj={0if j≠i1if j=iL_i(z)=\prod_{j\in[K],j\neq i} \frac{z-\omega_j}{\omega_i-\omega_j}=\begin{cases} 0 & \text{if } j\neq i \\ 1 & \text{if } j=i \end{cases}Li​(z)=∏j∈[K],j=i​ωi​−ωj​z−ωj​​={01​if j=iif j=i​.

Then every f(Xi)=f(ℓ(ωi))f(X_i)=f(\ell(\omega_i))f(Xi​)=f(ℓ(ωi​)) is an evaluation of polynomial f∘ℓ(z)f\circ \ell(z)f∘ℓ(z) at ωi\omega_iωi​.

If the master obtains the composition h=f∘ℓh=f\circ \ellh=f∘ℓ, it can obtain every f(Xi)=h(ωi)f(X_i)=h(\omega_i)f(Xi​)=h(ωi​).

Goal: The master wished to obtain the polynomial h(z)=f(ℓ(z))h(z)=f(\ell(z))h(z)=f(ℓ(z)).

Intuition:

  • Encoding is performed by evaluating ℓ(z)\ell(z)ℓ(z) at α1,…,αP∈F\alpha_1,\ldots,\alpha_P\in \mathbb{F}α1​,…,αP​∈F, and P>KP>KP>K for redundancy.
  • Nodes apply fff on an evaluation of ℓ\ellℓ and obtain an evaluation of hhh.
  • The master receives some potentially noisy evaluations, and finds hhh.
  • The master evaluates hhh at ω1,…,ωK\omega_1,\ldots,\omega_Kω1​,…,ωK​ to obtain f(X1),…,f(XK)f(X_1),\ldots,f(X_K)f(X1​),…,f(XK​).

Encoding for Lagrange coded computing

Need polynomial ℓ(z)\ell(z)ℓ(z) such that:

  • Xk=ℓ(ωk)X_k=\ell(\omega_k)Xk​=ℓ(ωk​) for every k∈[K]k\in [K]k∈[K].

Having obtained such ℓ\ellℓ we let X~i=ℓ(αi)\tilde{X}_i=\ell(\alpha_i)X~i​=ℓ(αi​) for every i∈[P]i\in [P]i∈[P].

spanX~1,X~2,…,X~P=spanℓ1(x),ℓ2(x),…,ℓP(x)span{\tilde{X}_1,\tilde{X}_2,\ldots,\tilde{X}_P}=span{\ell_1(x),\ell_2(x),\ldots,\ell_P(x)}spanX~1​,X~2​,…,X~P​=spanℓ1​(x),ℓ2​(x),…,ℓP​(x).

Want Xk=ℓ(ωk)X_k=\ell(\omega_k)Xk​=ℓ(ωk​) for every k∈[K]k\in [K]k∈[K].

Tool: Lagrange interpolation.

  • ℓk(z)=∏i≠kz−ωjωk−ωj\ell_k(z)=\prod_{i\neq k} \frac{z-\omega_j}{\omega_k-\omega_j}ℓk​(z)=∏i=k​ωk​−ωj​z−ωj​​.
  • ℓk(ωk)=1\ell_k(\omega_k)=1ℓk​(ωk​)=1 and ℓk(ωk)=0\ell_k(\omega_k)=0ℓk​(ωk​)=0 for every j≠kj\neq kj=k.
  • deg⁡ℓk(z)=K−1\deg \ell_k(z)=K-1degℓk​(z)=K−1.

Let ℓ(z)=∑k=1KXkℓk(z)\ell(z)=\sum_{k=1}^K X_k\ell_k(z)ℓ(z)=∑k=1K​Xk​ℓk​(z).

  • deg⁡ℓ≤K−1\deg \ell\leq K-1degℓ≤K−1.
  • ℓ(ωk)=Xk\ell(\omega_k)=X_kℓ(ωk​)=Xk​ for every k∈[K]k\in [K]k∈[K].

Let X~i=ℓ(αi)=∑k=1KXkℓk(αi)\tilde{X}_i=\ell(\alpha_i)=\sum_{k=1}^K X_k\ell_k(\alpha_i)X~i​=ℓ(αi​)=∑k=1K​Xk​ℓk​(αi​).

Every X~i\tilde{X}_iX~i​ is a linear combination of X1,…,XKX_1,\ldots,X_KX1​,…,XK​.

(X~1,X~2,…,X~P)=(X1,…,XK)⋅G=(X1,…,XK)[ℓ1(α1)ℓ1(α2)⋯ℓ1(αP)ℓ2(α1)ℓ2(α2)⋯ℓ2(αP)⋮⋮⋱⋮ℓK(α1)ℓK(α2)⋯ℓK(αP)](\tilde{X}_1,\tilde{X}_2,\ldots,\tilde{X}_P)=(X_1,\ldots,X_K)\cdot G=(X_1,\ldots,X_K)\begin{bmatrix} \ell_1(\alpha_1) & \ell_1(\alpha_2) & \cdots & \ell_1(\alpha_P) \\ \ell_2(\alpha_1) & \ell_2(\alpha_2) & \cdots & \ell_2(\alpha_P) \\ \vdots & \vdots & \ddots & \vdots \\ \ell_K(\alpha_1) & \ell_K(\alpha_2) & \cdots & \ell_K(\alpha_P) \end{bmatrix}(X~1​,X~2​,…,X~P​)=(X1​,…,XK​)⋅G=(X1​,…,XK​)​ℓ1​(α1​)ℓ2​(α1​)⋮ℓK​(α1​)​ℓ1​(α2​)ℓ2​(α2​)⋮ℓK​(α2​)​⋯⋯⋱⋯​ℓ1​(αP​)ℓ2​(αP​)⋮ℓK​(αP​)​​

This GGG is called a Lagrange matrix with respect to

  • ω1,…,ωK\omega_1,\ldots,\omega_Kω1​,…,ωK​. (interpolation points)
  • α1,…,αP\alpha_1,\ldots,\alpha_Pα1​,…,αP​. (evaluation points)
